title: keygenerator output schema v1
description: Machine-readable output emitted by `keygenerator` subcommands when --json is set. Stable across the v2.x major release line.

schema_version.description: Output schema version. Increments on breaking changes only. The current major-line schema is 1.

password.description: The generated credential. Omitted by the `entropy` subcommand (which consumes a password rather than producing one).

length.description: Length of the credential in characters (or for passphrase, number of words).

charset_id.description: Identifier of the source character set or word list. Versioned (e.g. "alphanum-v1"); appending "+excluded" indicates --exclude was applied.

charset_size.description: Cardinality of the charset or wordlist used for generation.

entropy_bits.description: Shannon entropy in bits of a uniformly drawn credential of the reported length over the reported charset (length * log2(charset_size)).

excluded_count.description: Count of distinct runes excluded from the charset via --exclude.

excluded_sha256.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 of the --exclude argument (omitted when no exclusion was applied). Allows audit logs to correlate excluded sets without echoing them.

required_classes.description: Comma-separated list of character classes the output is guaranteed to contain. For the `entropy` subcommand, lists observed classes instead.

algorithm.description: Identifier for the generation algorithm used.

version.description: Build identity: semantic version of the binary (e.g. "v2.0.0"). "dev" for unstamped development builds.

commit.description: Build identity: git commit SHA. "none" for unstamped builds.

build_date.description: Build identity: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp from the build pipeline.

request_id.description: RFC 4122 version-4 UUID generated for this invocation. Allows correlation across the stdout record and the audit log.

timestamp_utc.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp of generation, nanosecond precision.

warnings.description: Non-fatal advisories emitted by the generator (e.g. when --allow-weak bypasses the entropy floor or when a compatibility flag was used).

crack_time_estimates.description: Optional opt-in field (set with --show-crack-time on the CLI). Time-to-break estimates under named attacker profiles. Each estimate is informational and assumes uniform random search; real attacks may be faster or slower depending on KDF, hardware, and dictionary effects. Profile IDs are versioned and reflect 2024-2026 cracking capabilities.
